archetypes.name: CLI Command
archetypes.description: CLI command handlers and subcommand implementations.

analysisHints.description: CLI command modules handle user-facing terminal commands

archetypes.name: Component
archetypes.description: React/UI component files with JSX-returning functions.

analysisHints.description: Components have inflated call counts from hooks, state setters, and JSX rendering
High fan-in expected — config modules are widely imported

archetypes.name: Entry Point
archetypes.description: Module entry points, public APIs, and CLI entry files where uncalled exports are expected.

analysisHints.description: Uncalled exports are expected in entry points

archetypes.name: Gateway
archetypes.description: Re-export-heavy gateway modules that concentrate cross-package imports.

analysisHints.description: Gateway modules are expected to re-export heavily

archetypes.name: Hook
archetypes.description: React hooks — custom hooks encapsulating reusable stateful logic.

analysisHints.description: React hooks follow the use* naming convention

archetypes.name: Model
archetypes.description: Data models, schemas, and ORM definitions.

archetypes.name: Page
archetypes.description: Page-level components, views, or screens — top-level UI entry points for routes.

analysisHints.description: Page/view components orchestrate many sub-components and have inflated call counts

archetypes.name: Route Handler
archetypes.description: Server-side HTTP route handlers (Express, Hono, Koa, etc.).

archetypes.name: Route Module
archetypes.description: Framework convention route modules (Remix/React Router) with loader/action/default exports.

analysisHints.description: Convention exports are expected in route modules

archetypes.name: Schema
archetypes.description: Runtime validation schemas and data shape definitions (Zod, Yup, Joi, etc.).

analysisHints.description: Schema modules define runtime data shapes for validation

archetypes.name: Service
archetypes.description: Service layer modules — API clients, data fetching, and business logic orchestration.

analysisHints.description: Service modules orchestrate business logic and external calls

archetypes.name: Store
archetypes.description: State management stores and slices where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for state management modules

archetypes.name: Types & Constants
archetypes.description: Type definitions, constants, and enums — companion files, not logic.

analysisHints.description: Unidirectional coupling and high fan-in expected for type files

archetypes.name: Utility
archetypes.description: Shared utility, helper, and infrastructure modules where high fan-in is expected.

analysisHints.description: High fan-in is expected for utility modules

evidence.detail: Root-level CI pipeline entry point that orchestrates analysis and PRD health validation; exported as runCI and imported by cli.js
Shared help formatting utilities (formatMainHelp, searchHelp, formatTypoSuggestion, etc.) imported by both cli.js and tests — high fan-in utility module
Adaptive analysis service for agent decision-making — determines how the agent adjusts its strategy based on runtime signals
Review analysis service — analyzes agent progress and produces review-phase output for the autonomous loop
Spin detection service — identifies when the agent is looping without making progress
Stuck detection service — evaluates whether the agent is blocked and unable to proceed
Summary generation service — produces structured summaries of agent run state
Workflow analysis service — analyzes the current workflow state to inform agent lifecycle decisions
Agent completion handler — determines and processes task completion conditions in the agent loop
CLI-specific agent loop implementation — drives the interactive CLI variant of the agent execution cycle
Heartbeat monitoring service — tracks agent liveness and emits periodic status signals during long runs
Core agent loop orchestrator — the primary execution loop driving tool-use turns and state transitions
Shared lifecycle utilities — common helpers reused across loop, cli-loop, and heartbeat modules
Task display utilities — formats and renders task state for terminal output during agent runs
Token budget management service — enforces and tracks token limits across agent turns
Token usage tracking service — accumulates and reports token consumption across the agent session
Brief generation service — assembles the task brief passed to the LLM at the start of each agent run
Prompt construction service — builds structured system/user prompts for agent planning phases
Agent tool registry — defines and wires the tools available to the agent's tool-use loop
CLI help command handler — formats and outputs help text for the hench CLI
Guard command implementations — enforces command-level restrictions as part of the agent guard system
Guard type contracts — pure type/interface definitions (GuardConfig, PolicyLimitsConfig) with no runtime logic
Guard path utilities — path resolution and validation helpers used by the guard subsystem
Policy enforcement service — evaluates and applies guard policies to agent actions
PRD operations service — business logic for reading/writing PRD state, re-exported through rex-gateway
Explicitly the rex gateway module per CLAUDE.md — re-exports 8+ functions from rex with no added logic; all cross-package rex imports pass through here
Concurrent execution metrics utility — collects and exposes timing/throughput data for parallel process runs
Shell execution utility — low-level wrapper for spawning shell processes used across the agent
General process execution utility — shared abstraction over child_process for command execution
Process lifecycle service — manages start/stop/signal handling for spawned subprocesses
Cross-process concurrency limiter using PID-based lock files — shared infrastructure with no direct domain logic
Platform-aware system memory reader implementing a monitoring interface — pure infrastructure helper
Threshold-based execution gate that wraps the memory monitor — infrastructure utility with no business logic
Manages lifecycle of reusable Node.js worker processes with acquire/release semantics — stateful resource orchestration
Per-process RSS tracking with ring buffers and warning thresholds — metrics collection utility
Stateful FIFO queue with priority-ranked insertion that holds in-flight task state — high fan-in store pattern
Single formatting function that renders queue status as human-readable CLI output
Pure priority resolution logic combining CLI overrides, explicit priorities, and tag-derived priorities — no side effects
Self-contained glob pattern matcher implemented as a pure function — classic shared utility
Defines ToolGuard and ToolContext interfaces that establish the agent-core/tools boundary — no runtime logic
Central tool routing hub that maps tool names to implementations and manages zone coupling — orchestration logic
Shared subprocess exec wrapper with output formatting and timeout boilerplate — reused across multiple tool files
File operation tool suite (read/write/list/search) that enforces guards, tracks access, and checks sizes — stateful service
Single guard-checked git execution wrapper — thin tool adapter with no independent state
Integrates PRD domain into the agent tool layer via rex-gateway — orchestrates status updates and validation
Thin wrapper exposing a single guard-checked shell command tool
Coordinates post-run test execution strategy: locates co-located tests, scopes runs, and falls back to full suite
Validates task completion via git diff and test results — pure validation logic with no stored state
Anthropic SDK wrapper with retry logic, error classification, and streaming — canonical API client service
Detects available auth methods and validates credentials — stateless detection helpers
Spawns and parses the claude CLI binary with backoff and error classification — process-based provider service
Alternative vendor provider that spawns codex CLI and parses JSON output — mirrors cli-provider pattern
Factory function that selects between API and CLI providers based on available auth — utility factory, high fan-in expected
Centralized subprocess execution helpers (exec, spawn, timeout, buffers) shared across packages
Terminal help formatter with NO_COLOR/FORCE_COLOR support — pure formatting utility
Single toCanonicalJSON() function for deterministic serialization — minimal pure utility
Multi-vendor LLM client factory that unifies Claude and Codex behind a common interface
Loads and normalizes vendor config from .n-dx.json, handling legacy claude section migration
Vendor-neutral type definitions and re-exports only — no runtime logic
Shared deepMerge utility and project-level config helpers extracted from duplicate code in rex/hench
Resolves and exposes project directory paths — shared infrastructure with no business logic.
Defines interfaces/types for the provider abstraction layer; no runtime logic expected.
Manages provider registration and lookup — stateful orchestration with business logic.
Encapsulates session lifecycle for a provider — API client / session management logic.
Generates suggestions via the LLM — business logic orchestration over the provider API.
Tracks and aggregates token usage across calls — analytics/data-fetching service logic.
Handles the acknowledge phase of the analyze pipeline — domain business logic.
Guard/predicate that prevents invalid consolidations — pure logic helper.
Decomposes PRD items during analysis — core pipeline business logic.
Deduplication helper — stateless pure utility function.
Computes diffs between PRD states — stateless transformation utility.
Extracts structured data from LLM output — pipeline service with orchestration logic.
Validates file inputs for the analyze command — stateless validation helper.
Orchestrates guided/interactive analysis flow — top-level pipeline service.
Constructs reasoning prompts for modifications — LLM prompt/service logic.
Generates PRD proposals from analysis — core domain service.
Builds reasoning context for LLM calls — prompt/service orchestration.
Reconciles incoming proposals with existing PRD state — domain service.
Constructs reshape reasoning prompts — LLM service logic.
Scans project files to feed the analyze pipeline — data-fetching service.
Validates proposed modifications before applying — stateless validation helper.
Generates and formats CLI help text — shared presentation utility.
Implements MCP tool handler functions — server-side tool dispatch logic.
Implements the `rex mcp` CLI subcommand — command handler entry point.
Validates CLI input arguments — stateless validation helper.
Detects conflicts between recommendations — pure logic/predicate helper.
Creates PRD items from sourcevision recommendations — domain service with side effects.
Defines default workflow configuration/state — data-only configuration module.
Runs archetype classification analysis over the file inventory — analyzer service.
Classifies files by branch/work context — analyzer service with business logic.
Collects branch work data by comparing PRD state across git history — data fetching and business logic orchestration.
Filtering helper for branch work records — stateless transformation logic with high reuse potential.
Persists branch work records as JSON with Zod validation and read/write accessors — clear state management store pattern.
Generates deterministic architectural findings from call graph data — business logic that produces structured output.
Call graph analyzer using the TypeScript compiler API — complex analysis orchestration producing structured data.
File classification engine matching source files to archetypes via weighted signals — core business logic.
LLM API client bridge using @n-dx/llm-client — API client and data fetching layer.
Reads and extracts PRD completion state from rex for branch work tracking — data access and aggregation service.
React component catalog analyzer — scans files and produces structured component data.
Generates CONTEXT.md AI-readable codebase summary — output generation business logic.
Batch AI enrichment coordinator with retry logic — orchestrates Claude API calls for zone analysis.
Configuration constants for enrichment passes (batch sizes, concurrency limits, pass mappings) — pure config, no logic.
Stateless JSON parsing and finding extraction from AI responses — reusable parsing helpers with no side effects.
Per-zone AI enrichment engine — orchestrates incremental LLM-powered zone enrichment with parallelism.
Barrel re-export module that acts as stable public API for all enrichment sub-modules — classic entrypoint pattern.
Import graph analyzer — parses source files to build dependency edges and structured import data.
File inventory analyzer — crawls the filesystem and produces structured file metadata records.
Generates the llms.txt output file from analysis data — output generation and formatting service.
Pure Louvain modularity algorithm implementation — self-contained graph algorithm with no side effects, high reuse.
Reads and writes analysis manifest metadata (versioning, timestamps) — data management service.
Derives prioritized agent-ready next steps from findings — business logic that transforms raw data into actionable output.
Computes architectural risk metrics and emits findings — scoring and classification business logic.
Detects server-side routes from file patterns and exports — stateless detection helper used across the analysis pipeline.
Token usage tracking and aggregation utilities — shared helper for recording and querying LLM token consumption.
Merges pre-analyzed sub-repos into unified output — complex orchestration producing aggregated analysis artifacts.
Resolves cross-repo import crossings for workspace aggregation — dependency resolution business logic.
Detects pre-analyzed subdirectories and promotes their zones — workspace discovery and integration service.
Writes per-zone context.md and summary.json files — output emitter service for zone-scoped artifacts.
Zone detection and management orchestrator — drives Louvain clustering and zone lifecycle.
Shared help text definitions for the CLI — static content provider used by multiple commands, not a command handler itself.
CLI command handler for the MCP subcommand in the sourcevision CLI.
CLI command handler for the serve subcommand in the sourcevision CLI.
Service module that orchestrates PDF report generation from analysis output.
Utility that produces PR markdown output from a template; no business logic ownership.
Shared utility for merging data structures; high fan-in expected.
Shared path manipulation helpers; high fan-in expected.
Canonical JSON sorting helpers used across output generation; high fan-in expected.
Build script that configures the esbuild/bundler pipeline for the web package.
Dev-mode build script with watch/HMR configuration for local development.
Entry point module for the landing page bundle; wires up the landing page at load time.
Server-side caching service that aggregates and stores computed results.
Service that tracks concurrent execution metrics across agent runs.
Explicitly named gateway in CLAUDE.md; re-exports all sourcevision cross-package imports for the web server.
Service tracking incremental token/task usage over time.
Wires up and provides dependency instances for the MCP server; dependency configuration module.
Utility for port detection or resolution; no stateful business logic.
Service that diagnoses and reports on PR markdown refresh state.
Service that monitors and tracks process memory usage over time.
Explicitly named gateway in CLAUDE.md; re-exports all rex cross-package imports for the web server.
Service that builds and queries a server-side search index over PRD/inventory data.
Server startup entry point that initialises and launches the web server process.
Service that schedules periodic cleanup of stale usage records.
Service that manages WebSocket connections and message dispatch on the server.
Service that tracks WebSocket connection health and reports liveness metrics.
Shared constants and type definitions for data file paths; no runtime logic.
Bootstraps the client-side viewer application; initialises dependencies and mounts the app.
Pure physics/simulation utilities for graph layout; no UI or state ownership.
Low-level graph rendering helpers used by the viewer; not a standalone React component.
Client-side data fetching service that loads analysis data for the viewer.
Utility that throttles outbound messaging calls; pure rate-limiting logic with no state ownership.
Combines/batches WebSocket messages into coalesced groups; no logic layer, pure infrastructure utility with high fan-in from hooks and tests.
Throttles outgoing/incoming messages; infrastructure helper consumed by the messaging pipeline.
Deduplicates in-flight requests; shared infrastructure utility in the messaging subsystem.
Detects and reports renderer crashes; stateless monitoring helper consumed by performance subsystem.
Observes DOM timing metrics and feeds into polling-state; infrastructure utility with multiple callers.
Gates DOM updates based on performance thresholds; pure infrastructure helper.
Orchestrates feature-flag degradation tiers based on memory level; high fan-in from hooks, components, and polling.
Tracks JS heap snapshots and emits memory-level events; infrastructure monitoring utility.
Limits refresh rate to prevent thrashing; shared throttling helper in the performance layer.
Buffers and gates response delivery based on performance state; infrastructure utility.
Batches multiple pending updates into a single flush; performance infrastructure helper.
Dispatches accumulated ticks in a single batch; infrastructure helper in the polling pipeline.
Manages poller lifecycle (register/unregister/suspend/resume) and orchestrates tab-visibility integration; central business-logic coordinator with high fan-in.
Implements restart logic (suspend-all / resume-all / generation tracking) for the polling system.
Maintains global polling-source registry with subscribe/notify semantics; mutable shared state consumed by multiple polling and performance modules.
Wraps the Page Visibility API and emits change events; stateless infrastructure adapter.
Manages interval timers that drive polling ticks; low-level timer infrastructure.
Suppresses ticks while the tab is hidden; infrastructure gate utility in the polling pipeline.
Provides a migrateData helper for forward-compat between schema versions; single-purpose utility consumed by the loader.
Re-exports visualization helpers (colors, flow, metrics) and likely other shared viewer utilities; high fan-in barrel utility.
Defines ZONE_COLORS constant and color-lookup functions; primarily data/constant definitions with no side effects.
Pure functions that build ReactFlow node/edge data structures from zone and import graph data.
Provides meterClass helper for rendering metric meter elements; small pure utility.
Root-level CI utility script for PR validation checks; not a public API entry point or CLI command handler.
Root-level development utility script that refreshes build artifacts.
Root-level utility script that triggers a plan refresh cycle.
Root-level utility script that runs validation after a refresh.
Top-level server orchestration module; exports runWeb and process-management helpers consumed by cli.js — the public entry point for the dashboard and MCP server process.
